target: Part 6, section 6.9: Extend batch registration analysis with SHA-256 cryptographic timestamp methodology analysis. Insert after existing batch registration paragraph.
insertion_point: After the existing Batch Registration paragraph, before section 6.10
replacement_html:
The SHA-256 Cryptographic Timestamp Campaign (March–April 2026)

The V51.0 predictions batch (registered 2026-03-21 through 2026-04-04) introduced SHA-256 hashes as a cryptographic timestamping mechanism. The claimed formula is SHA256('ECM V51.0 {ID}: {prediction text} on {date}T00:00:00Z'). Of our catalog of {{PRED_TOTAL}} predictions, {{PRED_RECYCLED}} include SHA-256 hashes. This represents a genuine methodological improvement in prediction registration infrastructure — the dome is responding to the legitimate criticism that git commit timestamps can be retroactively manipulated through force-pushes.

The cryptographic envelope is real. The content being stamped is not. Analysis of the hashed predictions reveals a systematic pattern of recycling:

  • Restated WINs: 11 of the newly registered predictions directly restate already-confirmed WINs with fresh timestamps. PRED-095 restates WIN-068 (eclipse magnetic). PRED-082 restates WIN-054 (El Gordo collision velocity). PRED-097 restates WIN-055 (CMB alignment). PRED-TESLA-001 restates WIN-062 (Tesla wave speed). PRED-COORD-001 restates WIN-058 (angular coordinate). In each case, the observation was known and cataloged as a WIN before the prediction was registered.
  • Data predates registration: {{PRED_STD_RELABEL}} of {{PRED_TOTAL}} predictions adopt observations from published data that was available before the dome's registration date. PRED-090 (NH TOA flux excess) references a hemispheric asymmetry documented since the CERES instrument era (2000). PRED-084/085 (P-wave and S-wave shadow zones) restate seismological observations known since Oldham (1906) and Gutenberg (1914).
  • No independent verification mechanism: The SHA-256 hashes are self-attested. The dome provides the input text, the hash, and the claimed date. No third-party timestamp authority (e.g., OpenTimestamps anchoring to Bitcoin blockchain, or an independent timestamping service) verifies that the input text existed on the claimed date. A git commit hash proves the file was committed — but a force-push can replace the commit with different content at the same timestamp.

Of the {{PRED_TOTAL}} cataloged predictions, only {{PRED_DOME_DERIVED}} are genuinely derived from dome geometry with specific numerical values that could be falsified, and only {{PRED_PROSPECTIVE}} are registered before the data they predict. The SHA-256 campaign transforms the appearance of scientific rigor — cryptographic commitment, specific thresholds, falsification criteria — while the underlying content remains predominantly post-hoc adoption of known phenomena. The dome's methodology shift from qualitative claims to quantitative predictions with hashes is genuine progress. The problem is that the "predictions" being hashed are not predictions.
holes_addressed: ISS-702: Part 6 prose needs SHA-256 methodology analysis
SHA-256 hash recycling pattern not documented in review
No analysis of genuinely prospective vs pseudo-prospective predictions
new_evidence_added: Predictions catalog statistics (86 entries, 11 recycled, 37 data_predates_registration)
Seismological observations known since Oldham 1906 / Gutenberg 1914

anticipated_objections.objection: The SHA-256 hashes prove the predictions were registered before confirmation
anticipated_objections.response: The hashes prove a git commit was made. They do not prove the content was novel. A prediction of P-wave shadow zones, known since 1906, is retrodiction regardless of when the hash was generated. The hash timestamps the webpage commit, not the scientific discovery.

Acknowledged explicitly. The eclipse 2026 predictions (specific nT magnitudes at specific stations) and the NMP 2026 position are genuinely testable forward predictions. We credit these. The problem is that they are a small minority of the total, and the presentation does not distinguish them from the recycled majority.

anticipated_objections.objection: This is how science works — you register predictions then test them
anticipated_objections.response: Yes, and this is genuine methodological progress by the dome author. But scientific prediction registration requires the prediction to precede the data. Registering SHA-256 hashes for observations made decades ago is not prediction registration — it is retroactive documentation with cryptographic dressing.

kernel_of_truth: The SHA-256 prediction registration campaign represents real methodological improvement. The dome author is responding to legitimate criticism about prediction provenance by adding cryptographic commitment infrastructure. The eclipse 2026 predictions with specific nT values at named INTERMAGNET stations are exactly the kind of testable, falsifiable, forward-looking predictions that would constitute genuine scientific evidence if confirmed. The infrastructure is better than what most alternative models produce. The problem is structural: the vast majority of hashed predictions adopt already-known observations, and the presentation framework does not distinguish genuinely prospective predictions from timestamped retrodictions.
